coredata.dc:title: A novel fluorescence aptamer biosensor for trace Pb(II) based on gold-doped carbon dots and DNAzyme synergetic catalytic amplification
coredata.dc:creator: Li, Dan
Yuan, Xu
Li, Chongning
Luo, Yanghe
Jiang, Zhiliang
coredata.dc:description: Gold-doped carbon dots (CDAu) and carbon dots (CD) were quickly prepared by microwave irradiation procedure, using glucose as carbon source and HAuCl4 as doped reagent. Both CDAu and DNAzyme of Pb(II)-aptamer (Apt)-heme exhibit catalysis of the new fluorescence indicator reaction of H2O2-3,3′,5,5′-tetramethylbenzidine (TMB) to produce the oxidized TMB (TMBox) with a fluorescent peak at 404 nm. The Apt can be adsorbed on the CDAu surface to form a CDAu-Apt conjugate to inhibit the catalysis to cause the fluorescence intensity decreasing. The target for Pb(II) combined with the Apt to form stable G-quadruplex complex and free CDAu, in which G-quadruplex reacted with heme to form G-quadruplex/heme DNAzyme. Both the free CDAu and DNAzyme catalyzed in synergetic to produce TMBox, and the fluorescence enhanced greatly. Thus, a new and highly sensitive synergetic catalytic fluorescence method for the determination of 0.0005–0.46 μmol/L Pb(II) was established, with a detection limit of 0.25 nmol/L, its sensitivity is several orders of magnitude higher than most previously reported Pb2+ sensors. Compared with the CD, the CDAu has a quantum yield increase of 150%.
coredata.dcterms:subject: Gold-doped carbon dots
Aptamer
DNAzyme
Synergetic catalysis
Fluorescence
Pb(II)

xocs:item-toc-entry.xocs:item-toc-section-title: Molecular spectral characteristics and stability of CDAu2
Synergetic catalysis mechanism and Apt inhibition

xocs:funding.xocs:funding-agency: National Key Research and Development Program of China Stem Cell and Translational Research
National Natural Science Foundation of China

ce:caption.ce:simple-para: Principle of aptamer-mediated Au-doped carbon dots and G-quadruplex/heme synergetic catalytic amplifying fluorescence analytical platform for trace Pb(II).

Excitation spectra and fluorescence spectra. (A) Excitation spectra of 80 nmol/L Apt-Pb(II)-1.33 mg/mL CDAu2-0.33 mmol/L Tris-HCl system, the concentration of a-g was 0, 0.067, 0.47, 0.87, 1.27, 1.67 and 2.00 μmol/L Pb(II) respectively. (B) Fluorescence spectra of 80 nmol/L Apt-Pb(II)-1.33 mg/mL CDAu2-0.33 mmol/L Tris-HCl system, the concentration of a-g was 0, 0.067, 0.167, 0.2, 0.27, 0.33 and 0.4 μmol/L Pb(II) respectively.

Fluorescence spectra of the nanocatalytic system. (A) a-g: 0, 0.04, 0.14, 0.25, 0.39, 0.42, 0.59 mg/mL CDAu2-6.67μmoL/L H2O2-33 μmoL/L TMB-0.67 mmol/L Tris-HCl. (B) a-g: 0, 6.0, 16.0, 32.0, 41.0, 62.0, 82.0 nmol/L Apt-0.59 mg/mL CDAu2-6.67 μmol/L H2O2-33 μmoL/L TMB-0.33 mmol/L Tris-HCl. (C) a-e: 0, 0.3, 0.6, 1.3, 2.6 μmol/L Pb(II)-82 nmol/L Apt-7.5 × 10−8 mol/L HM-33 μmol/L TMB-6.67 μmol/L H2O2-0.33 mmol/L Tris-HCl. (D) a-g: 0, 0.012, 0.05, 0.1, 0.25, 0.35, 0.46 μmol/L Pb(II)-82 nmol/L Apt-7.5 × 10−8 mol/L HM-0.59 mg/mL CDAu2-6.67 μmol/L H2O2-33 μmol/L TMB-0.33 mmol/L Tris-HCl.

Mechanism of nanoenzyme gold-doped carbon dot and DNAzyme synergetic catalysis. (For interpretation of the references to color in this figure legend, the reader is referred to the Web version of this article.)
(A) Scanning electron micrograph of CDAu2. (B) Energy spectrum of CDAu2. (C) Infrared spectrum of CDAu2.
The quantum yields of carbon dots.
Comparison of nanocatalytic and aptamer inhibition characteristics.
Analysis characteristics of fluorescence detection of Pb2+ by CDAu and TMBOX probes.
Comparison of preparation procedures for gold-doped carbon dots.

row.entry: Microwave was used for preparation of CD and Au nanoclusters for 20 min, then both used to prepare CDAu by hydrothermal in 80。C for 2 h.

ce:caption.ce:simple-para: Comparison of some reported methods for Pb2+.

row.entry: UV detection of Pb2+ based on the color change of AuNPs and enzymatic cleavage of Pb2+.
Fast, good selectivity, but low sensitivity
The reaction products of Pb2+ react with Au3+ to produce AuNPs, which has an RRS effect, thus to detect Pb2+
Good selectivity, but low sensitivity and complicated process
Using the nano-simulated enzyme action of aptamer-AuNPs, combined with VBB and SERS to detect Pb2+.
High sensitivity and selectivity, but complicated operation
Pb2+ significantly expands the electrical response of gold nanofilms, enabling EM detection of Pb2+.
High sensitivity and selectivity, but complicated operation
Detection of Pb2+ based on the Fluorescence Quenching of CDs by Pb2+.
Combining the dual catalysis of carbon dot and DNAzyme, catalyze H2O2-TMB reaction to form TMBOX and detect Pb2+.
High sensitivita, good selectivity and simple operation

ce:table-footnote.ce:label: a
ce:table-footnote.ce:note-para: UV-Ultraviolet absorption spectroscopy; RRS-Resonance Rayleigh scattering; EM-Electrochemical method; FM-fluorescence method.

head.ce:title: A novel fluorescence aptamer biosensor for trace Pb(II) based on gold-doped carbon dots and DNAzyme synergetic catalytic amplification

ce:affiliation.ce:label: a
ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: Key Laboratory of Ecology of Rare and Endangered Species and Environmental Protection (Guangxi Normal University), Ministry of Education, Guilin, 541004, China

ce:correspondence.ce:text: Corresponding author. Key Laboratory of Ecology of Rare and Endangered Species and Environmental Protection (Guangxi Normal University), Ministry of Education, Guilin, 541004, China.;

sa:affiliation.sa:organization: Key Laboratory of Ecology of Rare and Endangered Species and Environmental Protection (Guangxi Normal University)

ce:abstract-sec.ce:simple-para: Gold-doped carbon dots (CDAu) and carbon dots (CD) were quickly prepared by microwave irradiation procedure, using glucose as carbon source and HAuCl4 as doped reagent. Both CDAu and DNAzyme of Pb(II)-aptamer (Apt)-heme exhibit catalysis of the new fluorescence indicator reaction of H2O2-3,3′,5,5′-tetramethylbenzidine (TMB) to produce the oxidized TMB (TMBox) with a fluorescent peak at 404 nm. The Apt can be adsorbed on the CDAu surface to form a CDAu-Apt conjugate to inhibit the catalysis to cause the fluorescence intensity decreasing. The target for Pb(II) combined with the Apt to form stable G-quadruplex complex and free CDAu, in which G-quadruplex reacted with heme to form G-quadruplex/heme DNAzyme. Both the free CDAu and DNAzyme catalyzed in synergetic to produce TMBox, and the fluorescence enhanced greatly. Thus, a new and highly sensitive synergetic catalytic fluorescence method for the determination of 0.0005–0.46 μmol/L Pb(II) was established, with a detection limit of 0.25 nmol/L, its sensitivity is several orders of magnitude higher than most previously reported Pb2+ sensors. Compared with the CD, the CDAu has a quantum yield increase of 150%.

ce:section.ce:para: Carbon dots (CD) are novel fluorescent material with good chemical stability [1], low toxicity, low cost, easy access to synthetic materials, easy functionalization and fluorescence up-conversion. Its preparation and analytical applications have received great attention [2–4]. The preparation of CD includes hydrothermal, solvothermal, electrochemical, microwave and electron beam lithography. Among them, hydrothermal synthesis is a classical procedure but time-consuming. And microwave-assisted (MW) pyrolysis has become the most feasible method for carbon dots preparation, which due to its low energy consumption, strong sustainability, short reaction time and high yield [5]. Li et al. used candle soot as precursor to prepare CD by combustion method, CD labelled single-stranded DNA (ssDNA) fluorescent probes to detect 0.5–10 μmol/L Hg2+, with a detection limit of 10 nmol/L [6]. However, CD fluorescence quantum yield and active sites were low. In recent years, the quantum yield and the active sites of CD were improved by hetero atom doping [7–12]. Gu et al. prepared fluorescent nitrogen-rich quantum dot (NRQDs) by solvothermal procedure at 115 °C for reaction of 2 h, and used to 0.15–10 μmol/L cysteine by fluorescence effect [13]. Cui et al. used methionine and as precursor to prepare a dual-function N–S doped carbon dots by hydrothermal procedure under 200 °C for 12 h, and was used to detect 12.5–1000 nmol/L Fe3+, based on the coordination reaction [14]. The citric acid and ethylene-diamine were used as precursors to prepare CD by microwave procedure, it was reduced AgNO3 to fabricated a carbon-modified silver nanoparticle (CDs-AgNPs) to determine 0.3–8 mmol/L Cu2+ by colorimetric method [15]. Using folic acid, glycerol and chloroauric acid as precursors, Au/N doped CD (CDAu/N) wee prepare by hydrothermal procedure at 180 °C for 12 h, the CDAu/N fluorescence probes were used to determine 0.118–41.86 μM Hg(II) [16]. Due to the different band structure of the metal atom doping, it can improve the optical properties of the carbon dots and generate new functions such as strong catalysis. In this study, we take only 10 min to get gold-doping carbon dots in the condition of 4.5 atm 140 °C by microwave method, and the carbon dots prepared by this method not only have highly catalytic effect, but also increase their quantum yield by 150%.

Aptamers (Apt) are a class of single-stranded oligonucleotides synthesized and screened in vitro [17]. It can specifically combine with target molecule such as metal ions, protein, small molecule etc. For the good stability and biocompatibility, easily synthesize and modify, it was used in biochemical analysis [18]. The application of nanomaterials has made great progress in fluorescence analysis. Anjali et al. synthesized 3,4-dihydroxy-1-phenylalanine (l-DOPA) protected gold nanoclusters (l-DOPA-AuNC), the coordination of Fe(III) ions and catechol groups on the surface of l-DOPA-AuNCs induces fluorescence quenching, and ascorbic acid (AA) can restored the fluorescence, 0.48–3.33 nmol/L Fe(III) and 32–156 μmol/L AA can be detected respectively [19]. Recently, coupled to nanoparticle with superior optical properties, Apt fluorescence nanoanalysis has been reported [20–22]. Li et al. used two Apts and fluorescence silver nanoclusters to measure 0.005–2 μmol/L human α-thrombin [20]. Qian et al. modified the Apt on the reduced graphene fluorescent quantum dots to detect 9.9–435 nmol/L Pb(II) [21]. Saberi et al. designed a complementary double-stranded structure DNA in which ATP Apts were labelled on the surface of magnetic nanoparticles, and the ligands were labelled on fluorescent carbon quantum dots to determine 0.003–5.0 nmol/L ATP [22]. The label-free Apt fluorescence method does not need to modify the fluorescent substance to bind the ligand, which has the advantages of simplicity, rapidity and high specificity [23]. Wang et al. established a fluorescence method for detecting 50–500 μmol/L isocarbophos and profenofos by Apt recognition of the target molecule, DNA will be dissociated from the double-stranded structure to enhance the fluorescent signal [24]. Li et al. established a fluorescence detection method for 10–80 ng/mL BPA, based on the fluorescence quenching effect of dispersed gold nanoparticles on CdTe quantum dots [25]. The guanine-rich DNA sequence can be folded to form a special G-quadruplex structure with the help of certain cations, and then combined with heme to produce a G-quadruplex/heme DNAzyme with peroxidase-like activity [26]. With unique advantages such as chemical stability, low cost, selectivity and real-time analysis capabilities, it has been successfully applied to the detection of metal ions. Based on the similar peroxidase activity of DNAzyme, we applied it to the H2O2-TMB reaction, combined with the catalytic effect of carbon dots on the reaction, to achieve the synergistic catalytic amplification of DNAzyme and carbon dots on the reaction. Catalytic amplification is one of the important ways to improve the sensitivity of analytical methods. It has been reported that the combination of cascade bioenzyme, metal nano-enzyme and DNAzyme catalytic amplification with photometry, fluorescence and resonance Rayleigh scattering spectroscopy [27,28]. Ju et al. constructed a colorimetric sensor using nitrogen-doped graphene quantum dots/silver nanoparticles (AgNPs-N-GQDs) as catalyst and used for the detection of 0.1–157.6 μm M glutathione (GSH) [29]. Cai et al. fabricated a fluorescence analytical platform for the detection of 2.0 × 10−6 -3.0 × 10−4 mol/L uric acid based on graphene quantum dots and G-quadruplex/heme DNAzyme [30]. However, there are no reports about aptamer-mediated carbon dots and G-quadruplex/heme synergetic catalytic amplifying fluorescence analytical platform for trace Pb(II).

Detection of ions are very important, especially for toxic metals [31]. Lead is a toxic heavy metal contaminant that can seriously affect human health [32]. Therefore, it is significant to establish a simple, rapid and reliable method for Pb2+ in environmental protection and human health. At present, there are several methods for detecting Pb2+ such as atomic absorption spectrometry (AAS), atomic fluorescence spectrometry (AFL), inductively coupled plasma mass spectrometry (ICP-MS) and spectrophotometry [33–36]. However, the application of these technologies is limited due to high measurement costs and/or low selectivity and sensitivity. So, it remains a challenge for the determination of trace lead ion (II) to design a new method with simplicity, sensitivity and selectivity. A label-free thrombin binding aptamer probe was reported for highly sensitive and selective detection of lead (II) ions by a resonance Rayleigh scattering [37]. Liang et al. reported a resonance Rayleigh scattering method for trace Pb, coupling the hydride generation with nanogold [35]. Wang et al. developed a DNA enzyme (17E DNAzyme) using unmodified gold nanoparticles (GNP) to determine 500 nmol/L Pb2+ by colorimetric detection [38]. A DNAzyme-based plasmonic nanomachine was reported for selective surface-enhanced Raman scattering (SERS) detection of lead ions via a particle-on-a-film hot spot construction [39]. In this paper, a new and highly sensitive synergetic catalytic amplifying fluorescence method was established for detection of 0.001–0.46 μmol/L Pb2+ by the new fluorescence probe of TMBOX. The results show that the fluorescence aptamer biosensor for Pb2+ we developed is simple, fast and convenient, and its sensitivity is several orders of magnitude higher than most previously reported Pb2+ sensors.

A 0.1 mol/L H2O2 (Xilong Science and Technology Co., Ltd., Shanghai, China), 50 mmol/L KOH, 50 mmol/L NaOH, 0.01 mol/L HAuCl4, 6 mmol/L HCl, 1 μmol/L Pb(II), 30.85 μmol/L lead ion nucleic acid aptamer (Apt) sequence: 5′-3′GGT TGG TGT GGT TGG (Handsome Biotechnology Co., Ltd.), diluted 100 times to 0.3085 μmol/L. A 0.1 mmol/L heme chloride (HM, storage: 2–8 °C, HBO 289-1 g, CAS: 16,009-13-5, Shanghai Source Leaf Biotechnology Co., Ltd., Shanghai, China): 0.00326 g heme chloride was weighed and dissolved in 100 mL ethanol solution (ethanol: water = 1:1). A pH 3.24 Tris-HCl buffer solution: add 0.5 mL 0.1 mol/L Tris solution and 0.55 mL 0.1 mol/L HCl in a 10 mL centrifuge tube and mix, then dilute to 10 mL to obtain pH 3.24 Tris-HCl buffer solution, 0.1 mol/L Tris, 0.1 mol/L HCl, 0.5 mmol/L 3,3′,5,5′-Tetramethylbenzidine (TMB, Storage: 2–8 °C, T818493-5 g, CAS: 54,827-17-7, Makclin Biochemical Technology Co., Ltd., Shanghai, China), 12 mg TMB was taken into a 100 mL volumetric flask at a volume ratio of ethanol: water = 6:4.

A model of DXR smart Raman spectrometer (Thermo Co., USA) with laser wavelength of 633 nm, power of 3.5 mW, slit of 50 μm, acquisition time of 5s, a model of Hitachi F-7000 fluorescence spectrophotometer (Hitachi High-Technologies Corporation, Tokyo, Japan), a model of TU-1901 double beam UV–visible spectrophotometer (Beijing Purkingje General Instrument Co., Ltd., Beijing, China), a model of KQ3200DB CNC ultrasonic cleaner (Kunshan Ultrasonic Instrument Co., Ltd., Kunshan, China) with an ultrasonic power of 150 W, working frequency of 40 KHz, a model of 79–1 magnetic stirrer with heating (Zhongda Instrumental Plant, Jiangsu, Nanjing, China), a model of HH-S2 electric hot water bath (Earth Automation Instrument Plant, Jintan, China), pH meter (Mettler Toledo Instrument Shanghai Co. Ltd., Shanghai, China), a model of Zetasizer Nano nanometer particle size and zeta potential analyzer (Malvern Instrument Co., Malvern, UK), a model of WX-6000 microwave digestion instrument (PreeKem, Shanghai, China), a model of Fourier transform infrared spectrometer (PerkinElmer, Shanghai, China), and a model of FD-1C-50 vacuum freeze dryer (Hangzhou Jutong Electronics Co., Ltd., Hangzhou, China) were used.

1 g of glucose and 0, 500 μL, 2 mL, 3 mL 0.01 mol/L HAuCl4 solution were added in 20 mL water respectively, numbered CDAu0-3. Then, it was transferred into a polytetrafluoroethylene autoclave that was irradiated by microwave with pressure of 4.5 atm and 140 °C for 10 min, to get a light yellow clear solution. A 1 mL of the prepared carbon dot solution was added to a10 mL tube, controlled the pH to 7.0 with 150 μL 50 mmol/L KOH solution, and diluted to the mark with water to obtain a 1.8 mg/mL CDs that calculated by the total carbon amount. All reagents were of analytical grade and the used water was ultrapure water.

A 40 μL 0.3085 μmol/L Apt, a certain amount of Pb(II) and 150 μL 1.0 × 10−6 mol/L HM solutions were added into a 5 mL marked test tube mixed well. After 10 min, a 200 μL 25 ng/mL CDAu, 100 μL of 5 × 10−4 mol/L TMB solution, 100 μL of pH 3.24 Tris-HCl buffer solution, 100 μL of 100 μmol/L H2O2 were added respectively, and then diluted to 1.5 mL with water and placed in the ultrasound instrument for irradiation 10 min. The reaction was stopped by ice-water cooling. The fluorescence spectrum was obtained by fluorescence spectrophotometer under the conditions of volt = 350 V, excited slit = emission slit = 5 nm, excitation = 275 nm, the fluorescence peak at 404 nm was measured as F, no Pb2+ was added as blank F0, and ΔF= F–F0 value was calculated.

On the one hand, using CDAu as fluorescence probe, a new and simple labelled-free Apt fluorescence method for the rapid determination of lead ions was established, based on the fluorescence enhancement of Apt-Pb(II)-CDAu system due to the labelled-free Apt reaction-mediated the CDAu probe. On the other hand, the catalytic function of free CDAu, coming from the Apt-Pb(II)-CDAu reaction, was used to speed up the redox reaction of H2O2 and TMB to form oxidation products TMBOX with strong fluorescence. Meanwhile, the produced G-quadruplex combined with heme (HM) to form DNAzyme that also catalyzed the H2O2-TMB fluorescence indicator reaction. Thus, a new and highly sensitive CDAu and DNAzyme synergetic catalytic fluorescence method for analysis of trace Pb2+ was established (Fig. 1 ).
H2O2 + CDAu = HO
H2O2 + DNAzyme = HO

The fluorescence properties of CDAu0-CDAu3 were investigated. Table 1 shows that CDAu2 has a strongest fluorescence peak at 445 nm with an excitation wavelength of 351 nm, with a biggest slope between the intensity and concentration. As the concentration increases, the intensity of the fluorescence peak increases (Fig. S1A). The fluorescence peak at 445 nm held constant when the excitation wavelengths change (Fig. S1B). This results indicate that the excited energy bands hold constant when excited wavelength changes, it is similar to the fluorescence of organic compound. As the Au content in CDAu0-CDAu3 increases, the fluorescence intensity increases. Because a strong chemical bond formed between the carbon dots and gold with rich electrons, which is beneficial to the plasmon resonance effect of Au atoms to makes the carbon dot fluorescence increase. The fluorescence intensity decreases after adding the excessive Au content such as CDAu3, which may be due to the excessive gold ions overlapping CD. Using quinine sulfate as a reference substance of quantum yield (QY = 0.54), the carbon dot QY was measured by the reference procedure [41]. According to the following formula, the QY of carbon dots was obtained.
Y
1
=
Y
2
∗
(
I
1
A
1
I
2
A
2
)
∗
(
n
1
n
2
)
2

In the above formula, Y was the QY, I was the integrated area of the fluorescence spectrum from 345 nm to 550 nm, A is the absorbance value, and n was the refractive index of the solution. The subscript 1 is the test object, and 2 is the reference substance quinine sulfate. To reduce the error, the absorbance value should be controlled below 0.05. Table 2 shows that the QY of CDAu2 was larger than the CD. According to the reference peocedure[42], the fluorescent lifetime of CDAu2 and CD was 3.5 μs and 2.2 μs respectively.

The fluorescence and excitation spectra of Apt-Pb(II)-CDAu0-3 systems were examined. The results show that when the emission wavelength (λem) was fixed at 445 nm, the most sensitive system of Apt-Pb(II)-CDAu2 produced three excitation spectrum peaks at 223 nm, 251 nm, and 351 nm, respectively (Fig. 2 A). When 251 nm is selected as the excitation wavelength to result octave peak is too close to the peak position of the emission spectrum, which brings certain difficulty to the measurement. Therefore, 351 nm was chosen as the excitation wavelength. The fluorescence emission peak of Apt-Pb(II)-CDAu2 system gradually increased at 445 nm peak intensity with the increase of Pb(II) concentration (Fig. 2B).

CDAu2 exhibited catalysis of H2O2 oxidization of TMB to form fluorescence product TMBOX that appeared a fluorescence peak at 404 nm (Fig. 3 A), the peak increased with the nanocatalyst concentration increasing. Upon addition of Apt, the peak decreased due to Apt inhibition of CDAu2 catalysis (Fig. 3B), the peak decreased with Apt concentration increasing, it is because the strongest intermolecular forces between the aptamer and CDAu2 with most Au atoms and stable, the aptamers encapsulate the CDAu2, which blocks the contact between the CDAu2 and the reactants, thereby suppressed the catalytic effect of the carbon dots [40] And the CDAu3 was unstable to aggregate. The fluorescence and excited spectra of Apt-Pb(II)-HM-systems were also considered. The DNAzyme of G-duplex/HM catalyzed the H2O2 oxidization of TMB to form TMBOX that exhibited two excitation spectra peaks at 240 and 279 nm, respectively. The later peak was chosen for use. The fluorescence spectra (Fig. 3C) show that a strong fluorescence peak appeared at 404 nm, the fluorescence signal was linear to the Pb (II) concentration, and was chosen for use. The fluorescence and excitation spectra of Apt-Pb(II)-HM-CDN/CDAu0-3-H2O2-TMB catalytic systems were considered (Fig. 3D, Fig. S2). The CDAu2 system produced a strongest fluorescence peak at 404 nm at an excitation wavelength of 275 nm, the fluorescence peaks gradually increased with increasing Pb(II) concentration, and was chosen for detection of Pb(II).

ce:section.ce:section-title: Molecular spectral characteristics and stability of CDAu2
ce:section.ce:para: The prepared CDAu2 was stand at room temperature for several days, which were measured the maximum value by Abs, RRS, Flu and SERS in different times. The CDAu2 was stable, with a relative standard deviation (RSD) 1.5–3.8% within 10% for 12 days (Table S1) by four molecular spectral methods. The resonance Rayleigh scattering (RRS) spectrum of CDAu2 was investigated experimentally with two RRS peaks at 295 nm and 450 nm (Fig. S3), the 450 nm is the characteristic RRS peak of CDAu2. With the concentration of CDAu2 increasing, the peak gradually increased. In the absorption spectra of CDAu2 (Fig. S4), an absorption peak at 425 nm is observed, and the peak value increased with the CDAu2 concentration increased. In the Raman spectra of VBB molecular probes in CDAu2 substrate (Fig. S5), a strongest peak at 1618 cm-1 was observed, and the peak intensity increased with the CDAu2 concentration increased. Results (Table S2) show that CDAu2 will aggregate in the NaCl solution. The SERS signal IAu2 at 1615 cm−1 raised to a gentle in different concentration gradient NaCl solution.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Synergetic catalysis mechanism and Apt inhibition

ce:section.ce:para: The CDAu2 was measured by transmission electron microscopy (TEM, Fig. 5 A) with an average particle size of about 10 nm The energy spectrum shows that CDAu2 mainly contains C and Au (Fig. 5B). The energy spectrum peak of C element appears at 0.2 keV, and the weight percentage is 16.0%. The Au peak appears at 2.20 keV with weighs 1.3%. It shows that the CD is doped with gold successfully.

According to the experimental method, 10 mL CDAu2 solution was dried by vacuum-freeze drying procedure to obtain a solid sample to do infrared spectrum. From the infrared spectrum (Fig. 5C), CDAu2 has absorption at 3367 cm−1, which indicating that there is free OH stretching vibration, the absorption peak at 2926 cm−1 is C–H stretching vibration of aldehyde group, CDAu2 has absorption at 1641 cm−1, which indicating the existence of a CC conjugated structure; the absorption peak of 1415 cm−1 may be a methylene group; the absorption peak of 1030 cm−1 may be a CO stretching vibration.

We studied the effect of pH on fluorescent signals in Apt-Pb(II)-CDAu2 system. The fluorescence signal changes when adding different pH buffer solution (Fig. S7), and pH 5.2 is the best. The results illustrated that the ΔF445nm value reached the maximum in the condition of 0.47 mg/mL CDAu2 and 82 nmoL/L Apt, respectively (Figs. S8–S9). The Apt-Pb(II)-HM-CDAu2-H2O2-TMB catalytic systems were studied. The effect of pH 2.7–4.5 and buffer solution concentration on the ΔF404nm was investigated respectively. The results showed that the ΔF404nm value reached its maximum when the pH was 3.24 and buffer solution concentration of 0.33 mmol/L. It was found that the TMBOX has fluorescence that was stronger than the CDAu2. In the same experiment, we optimized other conditions, 6.67 μmmoL/L H2O2 (Fig. S10), 0.33 mmol/L TMB (Fig. S11), 0.67 mg/mL CDAu2 (Fig. S12), 8.23 nmoL/L Apt (Fig. S13), 7.5 × 10−8 mol/L HM (Fig. S14), a 10 min irradiation time were chosen (Fig. S15).

The effects of coexisting substances of Apt-Pb(II)-CDAu2 system on the determination of 0.1 μmol/L Pb2+ were investigated by experiment. The results show that the relative error is within ±10%, 1000 times K+, Ca2+, Zn2+, Cl−, CO3 2−, Cu2+, NH4 +, 500 times Na+, Fe2+, Cr3+, NO2 −, S2O3 2−, PO4 3−, 100 times Ba2+, Mg2+, SO4 2−, Hg2+, Al3+, 50 times Co2+, Cr6+, and 10 times Co2+ did not interfere for the determination of Pb2+ (Table S3). The results indicate that the method has good selectivity. The effects of coexisting substances on the synergetic-catalytic fluorescence determination of 0.1 μmol/L Pb2+ were also investigated. The relative error is within ±10%, 1000 times K+, Ca2+, Na+, Zn2+, CO3 2−, Cu2+, NH4 +，500 times Al3+, Mg2+, Cr3+, S2O3 2−, Cl−，100 times Fe2+, Ba2+, Cr6+, Co2+, NO2 −, PO4 3− and 50 times SO4 2−, Hg2+ did not interfere for the determination of Pb2+ (Table S4). The results indicate that the catalytic method is selective than the Apt-Pb(II)-CDAu2 system, for example Co2+, Cu2+, Cr6+ and Hg2+.

A 10 mL urine sample was pipetted into a pyrex digestion tube and 5 mL of HNO3 solution was added. The tube was placed in a heating block and heated to just below boiling to reduce volume to about 0.5 mL. Then 0.5 mL 30% H2O2 was added and the mixture was heated to dryness. The residue was dissolved in l0 mL 1 mmol/L HNO3 solution to obtain the sample solution. The Pb content was determined according the procedure. The results (Table S5) show that the recovery rate was 96.4%–105%, and the relative standard deviation (RSD) was 2.1%–4.3%. This results were agreement with that of graphite furnace atomic absorption spectrometry. Two water samples were taken from the wastewater of the laboratory of Guangxi Normal University and the pond water of Guangxi Normal University. The Pb2+ content was determined according the procedure after filtering and processing. The results (Table S5) show that the recovery rate was 94.3–95.5%, and the relative standard deviation (RSD) was 0.08–0.1%. National standards stipulate that the maximum allowable discharge concentration in wastewater is Pb2+ ≤0.05 mg/L, which indicates that wastewater discharge in this area is within the scope of national standards.

In this paper, the synergetic functional gold-doped carbon dots were synthesized rapidly by microwave procedure, it is not only can be used for fluorescence probe but also utilized as catalyst to amplify analytical signal. Combined with the label-free Apt reaction of Pb2+, the free CDAu and G-quadruplex/heme DNAzyme synergetic-catalysts formed in the analytical system. Based on the synergetic catalysis of H2O2-TMB indicator reaction to form TMBOX with strong fluorescent signal, a new synergetic catalytic fluorescence method was established to assay Pb(II) without labelled aptamer and CDAu, which has the advantages of high sensitivity, high selectivity, simplicity and rapidity. Cascade di-bioenzymes, bioenzyme and nanoenzyme have been used to amplify analytical signal, the former catalysis affects the latter to cause un-stability. This disadvantage of the cascade was overcome by the synergetic catalysis that was verified by our study, and it is a new strategy to improve sensitivity, selectivity and reproducibility in enzyme and nano-analysis.

Dan Li: Writing - original draft. Xu Yuan: Writing - original draft. Chongning Li: Formal analysis. Yanghe Luo: Funding acquisition, Conceptualization, Methodology. Zhiliang Jiang: Funding acquisition, Conceptualization, Methodology.

ce:acknowledgment.ce:para: This work was supported by the National Key R&D Program of China (2018YFD0901003) and National Natural Science Foundation of China (No. 21667006, 21767004).

ce:section.ce:para:
The following is the Supplementary data to this article:
Multimedia component 1 Multimedia component 1

ce:section.ce:label: Appendix A
ce:section.ce:para: Supplementary data to this article can be found online at https://doi.org/10.1016/j.jlumin.2020.117056.
